pattern: Dividing the length by the number of parts gives every part a base size, and the remainder tells you exactly how many of the earliest parts get one extra node.
hints: First find the total length; the sizes are almost equal, differing by at most one, with the larger parts coming first.
Integer division of the length by k is the base size and the remainder is how many leading parts get one more node.
Walk the list cutting off each part after its computed size, severing the next pointer so the part ends cleanly, and pad with empty parts when the list runs out.

edge_inputs.description: more parts than nodes, trailing parts are empty
uneven split where earlier parts are larger
empty list split into several empty parts
single part containing the whole list
length divides evenly so all parts are equal
remainder of one puts the extra node only in the first part
k equals length, every part has one node

stress.description: random list length 0..1000 split into a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, min(size, 1000))
    arr = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [arr, k]
stress.note: covers k both larger and smaller than the list length

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def splitListToParts(self, head, k):
        vals = []
        node = head
        while node:
            vals.append(node.val)
            node = node.next
        n = len(vals)
        base, extra = divmod(n, k)
        result = []
        idx = 0
        for i in range(k):
            sz = base + (1 if i < extra else 0)
            chunk = vals[idx:idx + sz]
            idx += sz
            head_part = None
            for v in reversed(chunk):
                head_part = ListNode(v, head_part)
            result.append(head_part)
        return result
